etymology.description: semantic 矢(矢) (shǐ, "arrow") + phonetic 㠯 -> particle indicating completed action
etymology.interpretation: 矢(矢) (shǐ, "arrow") is used as a semantic guide, suggesting a sense of direction or completion, while 㠯 provides the phonetic contribution. Together, they form a particle used to indicate the completion of an action or a finalized state.

examples.chinese: 我终於到达目的地矣(我終於到達目的地矣)。
examples.english: I have finally reached the destination.

examples.chinese: 一切皆已成矣(一切皆已成矣)。
他离开多时矣(他離開多時矣)。
examples.english: He has been gone for a long time.
